title: Authors and Citation
text: Darwish N (2026). metaExtra: Extra Utility Functions Meta-Analysis. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/NourEdinDarwish/metaExtra.
code: @Manual{,   title = {metaExtra: Extra Utility Functions for Meta-Analysis},   author = {Nour Edin Darwish},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/NourEdinDarwish/metaExtra}, }

title: Extra Utility Functions for Meta-Analysis
text: metaExtra provides collection extra utility functions meta-analysis R, designed complement meta package. currently includes: forest_save() saves meta forest plot disk automatically calculated optimal dimensions. forest_dims() calculates optimal width height meta forest plot.

title: Extra Utility Functions for Meta-Analysis
text: can install development version metaExtra GitHub :

title: Calculate forest plot dimensions — forest_dims
text: Extracts exact width height meta forest plot internal grid layout. primarily used determine optimal dimensions saving forest plot disk.

title: Calculate forest plot dimensions — forest_dims
text: x object class meta (e.g., meta::metacont(), meta::metabin(), meta::metagen()). ... Additional arguments passed underlying forest method (e.g., meta::forest.meta(), meta::forest.metabind(), meta::forest.metacum(), meta::forest.metainf()). units Units returned width height. One "", "cm", "mm". Defaults "".

title: Calculate forest plot dimensions — forest_dims
text: named list elements: width: width forest plot specified units. height: height forest plot specified units.

title: Calculate forest plot dimensions — forest_dims
text: Rather using guesswork manual row counting, forest_dims() captures forest plot true graphics object extracts precise width height directly underlying structure. mathematically measures actual rendered components, highly robust. works seamlessly forest plot configuration.

title: Calculate forest plot dimensions — forest_dims
code: # Create a simple meta-analysis object m <- meta::metagen(   TE = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.3),   seTE = c(0.2, 0.3, 0.15),   studlab = c("Study A", "Study B", "Study C") )  # Get dimensions in inches (default) forest_dims(m) #> $width #> [1] 8.768552 #>  #> $height #> [1] 3.2 #>   # Get dimensions in centimetres forest_dims(m, units = "cm") #> $width #> [1] 22.27212 #>  #> $height #> [1] 8.128 #>

what: Save a forest plot to disk — forest_save
title: Save a forest plot to disk — forest_save
text: Saves meta forest plot disk dimensions automatically calculated forest plot's internal grid layout via forest_dims().

title: Save a forest plot to disk — forest_save
code: forest_save(   x,   filename,   ...,   device = NULL,   path = NULL,   width = .width,   height = .height,   units = c("in", "cm", "mm", "px"),   dpi = 300,   device_args = NULL )

title: Save a forest plot to disk — forest_save
text: x object class meta (e.g., meta::metacont(), meta::metabin(), meta::metagen()). filename File name create disk. ... Additional arguments passed underlying forest method (e.g., meta::forest.meta(), meta::forest.metabind(), meta::forest.metacum(), meta::forest.metainf()). device Device use. Can either device function (e.g. png), one "eps", "ps", "tex" (pictex), "pdf", "jpeg", "tiff", "png", "bmp", "svg" "wmf" (Windows ). NULL (default), device guessed based filename extension. path Path directory save forest plot : path filename combined create fully qualified file name. Defaults working directory. width, height Forest plot size units expressed units argument. Defaults .width .height, auto-calculated dimensions forest_dims(). can use .width .height expressions (e.g., height = .height * 1.2) adjust forest plot size. set NA, size current graphics device used. units One following units width height arguments expressed: "", "cm", "mm" "px". Defaults "". dpi Plot resolution. Defaults 300. device_args named list additional arguments passed directly underlying graphics device (e.g., list(bg = "transparent", pointsize = 12)).

title: Save a forest plot to disk — forest_save
text: named list (returned invisibly) elements: file: Full file path. width: Forest plot width. height: Forest plot height. units: Units width height. dpi: Forest plot resolution.

title: Save a forest plot to disk — forest_save
text: auto-calculated dimensions exposed width height arguments special variables .width .height. can reference mathematical expressions easily adjust forest plot's size relative optimal dimensions:
code: # Auto dimensions (default) forest_save(m, filename = "plot.png")  # 20% taller and 2 units wider than the auto-calculated dimensions forest_save(m, filename = "plot.png", width = .width + 2, height = .height * 1.2)  # Explicit override forest_save(m, filename = "plot.png", width = 10, height = 8)

title: Save a forest plot to disk — forest_save
code: m <- meta::metagen(   TE = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.3),   seTE = c(0.2, 0.3, 0.15),   studlab = c("Study A", "Study B", "Study C") )  # Save with auto-calculated dimensions tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") forest_save(m, filename = tmp)  # 20% taller than auto-calculated tmp2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".pdf") forest_save(m, filename = tmp2, height = .height * 1.2)  # Explicit dimensions in centimetres tmp3 <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") forest_save(m, filename = tmp3, width = 25, height = 15, units = "cm")

what: metaExtra: Extra Utility Functions for Meta-Analysis — metaExtra-package
title: metaExtra: Extra Utility Functions for Meta-Analysis — metaExtra-package
text: Provides extra utility functions complement 'meta' package. Includes functions automatically calculating optimal dimensions saving forest plots disk.

title: metaExtra: Extra Utility Functions for Meta-Analysis — metaExtra-package
metaExtra (development version)
text: New forest_save() saves meta forest plot disk automatically calculated ideal dimensions forest plot’s internal grid layout. New forest_dims() calculates optimal width height meta forest plot saving.
